item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it's copyrighted art by John Van Hamersveld. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish Track 1 was not redacted. The rest is so creepy and disturbing, I'd love to see it. Track 6 also reminds me a bit of the Scarlet King.
+1, it made me uncomfortable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not gonna lie, I wasn't expecting the grateful dead. I was thinking of some sort of vengeful revanant. But after that song about encouraging duo use and the Sun, I can see that they're quite hateful indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pure awesomeness. Excellent job in not just twisting the lyrics, but in the imaginative variation of their subject matter.

Bonus points for deftful elision as well. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i like the scp, but im not a big fan of total redaction. it doesnt even give me a creepy idea of what track one does or if anything happened to listeners.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if "satanic music", but it's surely creepy…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love almost everything about this, but no vote because

Addendum 1398-01a: [REDACTED]

Just…I find these total "NO YOU CANNOT SEE" redactions to complete kill it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy shit this is awesome.

The best part is the altered lyrics for "Truckin'". That actually sounds like something I'd write.

Upvoted with the fury of a thousand Norse gods.

item.title: Re: sensitivity to the anomaly
item.content:encoded:
Thanks. The fact that Bright immediately knew where my handle came from is yet another confirmation I'm in the right place.

So, I'm thinking you change this:

The anomalous properties of SCP-1398 manifest when SCP-1398-1 is played in the presence of individuals capable of perceiving the anomalies.

Which is kind of tautological, to this:

The probability of an individual being susceptible to the anomalous properties of SCP-1398-1 is in direct proportion to that individual's familiarity with nonanomalous recordings of the same material.

Edit: Of COURSE probability is the right word here. Much more clinical than "chance".

item.title: Fuck, yes.
item.content:encoded: This SCP is brilliant, and it creeped me out. Also, kudos on using thanatophobia, both because it makes the song seem.. particularly disturbing, and because it kinda happened to me a little at a particularly bad time.

item.title: Re: sensitivity to the anomaly
item.content:encoded: Thanks much for the upvote; also glad you've joined us.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upon showing this to my Grateful Dead fan friend, he mentioned that the way you wrote the anomalous lyrics sounded really close to the song, and they really impressed him. So good on that one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The D Class and Level 1 personnel were more likely to perceive the anomaly than the civilian subjects, yes.

Whether that was dependent upon subjects having or not having criminal tendencies, whether or not they had heard any grateful dead in the last year, whether or not they were wearing nametags at the time of testing or some other criteria is up to you.

item.title: sensitivity to the anomaly
item.content:encoded:
I took this paragraph to mean that the more familiar a listener was with the original album, the more likely they would consciously notice the anomalous lyrics. So, if you (somehow) hadn't heard the songs before, you wouldn't hear anything out of the ordinary. If you're a deadhead on the other hand, you'd first think "hey, that doesn't sound like Sugar Magnolia", and then you'd tune into the lyrics… I thought that was really neat, and didn't notice that maybe it was phrased a little weirdly.

By the way - I'm not saying this was the best SCP on the site or anything, but it was the article that finally pushed me over the edge to join. You're my first upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Limited testing of civilian subjects has been approved … Out of 167 test subjects, only 3 were able to perceive anomalies during SCP-1398-1 playback.

So people with criminal tendencies are more likely to be able to perceive the anomaly? Since a much greater percentage of the D-class test subjects were could perceive it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this. It's sufficiently freaky, wigs me out personally, and while it has a really scary effect, it's not like "OMG SUPER DEATH RECORD," so that's good.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, that's the one! Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That could be either a great -J or an excellent short tale.
I would love to see either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, Scroton. I think I can finally get one of my Grateful Dead-loving friends into SCPs by showing him this one. Also, great SCP in and of itself. Glad to see you're successfully creating new things! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The rewrites are very clever, even though I don't know the original, and were enough for a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found it refreshing that you effectively lead with the spooky total redaction that others would leave for the final track. Nice change of pace. I only knew a couple of the other tracks tracks from this album, but felt a compulsion to go listen to The Golden Road immediately, and repeatedly, until I begin to hear alternate lyrics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ehh…the cock-blocking redaction in the addendum really gets my goat. However, it's well-written, and better than my SCP that makes you crazy to death. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't help thinking that, among Foundation personnel, "they grow on you" is probably not intended as a recommendation :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here you go

+ The Album
- The Album

1. The Golden Road (To Unlimited Devotion)
2. Truckin'
3. Rosemary
4. Sugar Magnolia
5. St. Stephen
6. Uncle John's Band
7. Casey Jones
8. Mexicali Blues
9. Turn On Your Love Light
10. One More Saturday Night

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice. I was never a big fan of the Dead, but this makes me want to get this album from my sister to compare and sing along.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this - it's much better than I expected. It's coherent, thematic, and the redaction actually works.

I'm kinda hoping there's an anomalous copy of Steal Your Face out there, somewhere… D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks much sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you get a chance to, you should check out some of their albums. They grow on you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. I was worried the large redaction might not work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just did. It looks like site 19 is the catchall, then?

Edit: Went ahead and changed the housing site to site 19.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have you checked this list http://www.scp-wiki.net/secure-facilities-locations ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed, with the exception of the site part. I'll probably fix that later. The reason I blacked out the site is because I didn't want to step on anyone's toes or create a site just to house this.

Is there a "everything that's easy to contain goes here" site?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good stuff.
A few minor nitpicks:

I'm not sure there's a reason to black out the Site. Personally, I don't really mind, but that does get frowned upon at times.
Discoveries where Agents just happen to stumble across anomalies bug me. Just a bit too convenient.

Class 1

If you mean staff, it's usually Level 1.
And this:

standard 12-inch (30 cm) LP record

This I'm not sure about. I'm a record collector from a metric using country and I've never heard of centimetres being used when describing vinyl records. Vinyl is always referred to in inches. Not a problem really, but that just read a bit weird to me, because I'm not used to that. Doesn't mean that the Foundation wouldn't still put it in, though.
All of these are of course minutiae and don't hold me from upvoting. The reason I'm pointing these out, is that reading small things like these make my mind wander and distract me from an article. More a problem of mine than of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was actually thinking along those lines for an SCP-J I wrote up some notes for; it's basically a Foundation maintenance personnel who always gets +20 to his psychic throws.

+ Spoilers
- Spoilers

Maintenance Log 234927

J: Cut it out man, I gotta fix this camera you broke.

SCP-███ begins vibrating and humming audibly, coils of an unknown energy arcing from it's base.

J: Listen guy, I don't care what happens in strange eons, I'm not going anywhere near you. Cut it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would be kinda kickass, IMO. Not here, but somewhere, people should have to beat a DC to not be affected by an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wrestled with that line; I couldn't figure out a way to word it and keep the meaning that wouldn't make it clunkier. I considered putting "perceptive individuals" but for it mentioning that for the first time in the article it sounded like they have to roll a d20 to see if they can hear it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kiiiinda. This is my first new SCP in quite some time. I was originally part of the SCP thing when it wasn't on wikidot, then came back a little while ago.

Recently I attempted two rewrites of my old scps that were (rightfully so) universally hated (the re-writes, that is.) They basically served as the "crash and burn and learn" phase that a lot of new writers go through here, albiet with much unneeded drama caused by me.

If you want to check the SCPs I had a hand in they are scp-071, which has since undergone a great re-write by Raven Mackenzie and the much contested scp-579 which underwent a re-write by Sophia Light in my absence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry if this sounds too nitpicky, but this sentence:

 The anomalous properties of SCP-1398 manifest when SCP-1398-1 is played in the presence of individuals capable of perceiving the anomalies.

Reads sort of clunkily to me. Seems like it would just make more sense to mention that some people can't perceive the anomaly immediately or at all.

Might just be me though.

Still upvoting because it's a neat/weird article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like this one pretty good. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are you thinking of scp-1666?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, it's a record that drives you crazy, but for some reason I really like the way this is written. One of the few times that I really want to know what's behind some of the redaction. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I must ask, is this your first SCP? If not, would you point me to your others?
Signed,
Avid Fan

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thrilling read. I love it when a great SCP has long addenda, as your does.
It puts me in mind of…Stephen King's The Stand and The Dark Tower….maybe this pressing of the record
comes from The Wasteland where The Dead were the minstrels of The Crimson King.

And of course, I was ensorceled at reading this one

Song directed at the sun, urging it to enter a supernova state.

The idea of (in very deadpan fashion) writing that a song is exhorting the Sun to commit suicide, well, I could live a thousand years and never come up with anything better

I regret I have but one vote to …. you know the rest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
see Addendum 1398-01a.
Addendum 1398-01a: [REDACTED]

Hey, yo. :(

Although I don't know the band itself, I gave a listen to some of the songs and yeah, if they had lyrics changed, they would, in fact, be pretty creepy. Looks like another "satanic music"; it reminded me of a certain SCP that was very similarly written, although I can't seem to find it, I think it was a Ozzy Osborne LP, not sure though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I barely know anything about the Grateful Dead aside from the time I saw them in 2004 after winning concert tickets on the radio (and most of the songs they played were covers anyway), so i'm in no position to really judge this SCP on its merits.

It's well-written, though, and it's damned weird, and interesting, and somehow I think Jerry Garcia would approve, so +1.

item.title: For those that aren't familiar with the music
item.content:encoded:
Somehow I don't think this SCP will do so very good. We'll see I guess.

+ For those that aren't familiar with the music in the SCP album
- For those that aren't familiar with the music in the SCP album

1. The Golden Road (To Unlimited Devotion)
2. Truckin'
3. Rosemary
4. Sugar Magnolia
5. St. Stephen
6. Uncle John's Band
7. Casey Jones
8. Mexicali Blues
9. Turn On Your Love Light
10. One More Saturday Night
